popularStory.headline: Cockayne Families
popularStory.description:
Is there another Old Derbshire family with such memorabilia for the historian, with so many vicissitudes and so many dramatic episodes, as the Cokaynes? 

Derbyshire provides much of the finest scener …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Cockayne於1325年出生在英國德比夏爾阿希布恩時，他的父親Sir John Cokayne Esquire25歲，母親Lettice Withers21歲。他於1356年與Cecelia Vernon結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1372年在他的家鄉享年47歲，埋葬在英國德比夏爾阿希布恩。

nameMeanings.name: Cockayne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands and Yorkshire): nickname for a dreamer, from Middle English cokayne ‘cloud-cuckooland’, the name of an imaginary paradise (Old French (pays de) cocaigne, from Middle Low German kōkenje, a diminutive of kōke ‘cake’, since in this land the houses were thought to be made of cake). It may also be a variant of Cocking or from the unflattering Middle English nickname cokin (Old French coquin) ‘disreputable person’.

variants.name: Cocking
meanings: habitational name from Cocking in Sussex. The placename denoted ‘(the settlement of) Cocking's people’, from the Old English personal name Cocca + the group-name suffix -ingas.
patronymic from either of two Middle English personal names, Cok(k)ing (Old English Coccing) or Cokin (probably Old French in origin), both of which are pet forms of Cocke 2.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John Cockayne was born in 1325, in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England, his father, Sir John Cokayne Esquire, was 25 and his mother, Lettice Withers, was 21. He married Cecelia Vernon in 1356. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1372, in his hometown, at the age of 47, and was buried in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Cockayne est né en 1325, à Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Cokayne Esquire, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lettice Withers, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Cecelia Vernon en 1356. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1372, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Cockayne 1325 in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Cokayne Esquire 25 und seine Mutter Lettice Withers 21. Er hat 1356 Cecelia Vernon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1372 im Alter von 47 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Ashbourne, Derbyshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: John Cockayneが1325年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアアシュボウルンで生まれた時，父Sir John Cokayne Esquireは25歳，母Lettice Withersは21歳であった。1356年にCecelia Vernonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1372年に故郷で47歳で死去し，イギリスデルブイシュアイアアシュボウルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Cockayne(이)가 1325년 영국 더비실르 애스h보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Cokayne Esquire(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lettice Withers(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1356년 Cecelia Vernon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1372년 그의 고향에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 더비실르 애스h보우른에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Cecelia Vernon em 1356. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1372, em sua cidade natal, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Ashbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Cockayne родился в 1325 г. в местности ашбурн, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Cokayne Esquire было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lettice Withers, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Cecelia Vernon в 1356 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1372 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности ашбурн, дирбишир, Англия.
